title: Render Supabase DSN Repair For Hosted Mutation-Seam DB Reads
objective: Repair the existing Render mutation-seam service database connection so DB-backed approval and schedule reads stop returning 500, while preserving the already-green hosted PM intake path and all production-write guardrails.
active_role: Desktop Codex Authenticated Hosted Executor / Technical Repo Authority Review

source_floor.meaning: Current clean-main after PM Lane 041A/041B closeouts were committed and Olares fast-forwarded.

current_state: Render access is not the blocker; the authenticated dashboard path already allowed service inspection, root directory repair, redeploy, and blocker classification.
Existing Render root directory is repaired to apps/mutation-seam.
Existing Render service is deployed from clean-main.
Hosted PM intake endpoints return 200 for project-import-candidate, project-import-admission-plan, project-import-approval-contract, and project-import-approval-storage-plan.
Remaining deployed mutation-seam failures are DB-backed approval/schedule reads returning 500.
Render logs classify the remaining failure as Supabase pooler ECIRCUITBREAKER from too many authentication failures.
No SQL, schema migration, secret rotation, import mutation, or business-state mutation has been performed.
allowed_scope: Confirm the correct SEAM_DATABASE_URL value from an authorized secret source without printing or committing the secret value.
Compare the hosted Render SEAM_DATABASE_URL against the authorized value using secret-safe evidence only, such as key presence, hostname/project reference, or redacted/fingerprint comparison.
Update the existing Render service SEAM_DATABASE_URL only if it is stale or wrong.
Let the Supabase pooler authentication circuit breaker cool down if necessary.
Restart or redeploy the existing Render service apex-platform-mutation-seam after the env update or after confirming the existing value is already correct.
Rerun the deployed mutation-seam smoke with --include-pm-intake and the paired PM intake hosted smoke.

Publish a closeout handoff with exact command results, secret-safe DSN confirmation method, Render deploy/restart evidence, PM intake proof, DB-backed approval/schedule proof, blocker classification if still red, and guardrail confirmation.
not_allowed: No secret value may be printed, pasted into a repo file, committed, logged into a handoff, or summarized in plaintext.
No Supabase password rotation unless a later explicit stakeholder approval admits rotation.
No project, workpackage, task, issue, work order, assignment, schedule, status, durable field record, production tracking write, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.
If the correct DSN cannot be confirmed from an authorized source, stop and close out with a blocker instead of guessing.
validation_steps: corepack pnpm --dir "C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform" --filter @apex/operations-web exec node scripts/smoke-pm-intake-hosted.mjs --operations-web-base-url https://operations.apexpowerops.com --mutation-seam-base-url https://mutation-seam.apexpowerops.com --timeout-ms 20000
Render log inspection must show whether Supabase pooler ECIRCUITBREAKER/authentication failures are gone, cooling down, or still present.
success_criteria: Hosted mutation-seam health and root return 200.
All four PM intake reads remain 200.
DB-backed approval queue and schedule reads no longer return 500, or any remaining 500 is precisely reclassified with logs after secret-safe DSN verification.
Paired PM intake hosted smoke remains failed=0.
No production write or authority widening occurred.
next_recommended_lane: After DSN repair closeout, coordinator should accept/reject the closeout and then continue PM Lane 123 local exception review and PM decision detail body controls if approval/import authority remains blocked.
